description: X-ray diffraction patterns of Fe-30Mn-10Al-1.5C-xBe (x = 0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5 wt%) low-density steel strips after hot rolling at 1050 °C, illustrating the phase evolution with increasing beryllium content. The 0Be sample shows characteristic austenite (γ) peaks indexed as (111), (200), (220), (311), and (222). At 0.5Be, the pattern remains predominantly austenitic with no observable ferrite peaks. Upon increasing Be to 1.0 wt%, distinct δ-ferrite (200) reflections appear alongside Be-Fe intermetallic compound peaks including Be11Fe (112, 212) and Be2Fe. The 1.5Be sample exhibits the most complex phase assemblage with simultaneous presence of austenite, ferrite, Be11Fe, and Be2Fe phases, indicating that excess Be precipitates as intermetallic compounds when the austenite matrix reaches saturation. The systematic emergence of ferrite and Be-rich phases with increasing Be content confirms the transition from single-phase to dual-phase microstructure, supporting the proposed solid solubility limits and precipitation sequence in Be-bearing low-density steels.
